Thursday night Farmsister and I picked up Mom and went over to the Brown’s taproom at Walloomsac for dinner. It was Farmsister’s first dinner sitting in a restaurant since the onset of the plague. (I took a photo of her eating her First Hot French Fry!!!!!) It was lovely. the service was deadly slow but we didn’t mind much, it was novel to be out together. Mom discovered at the end that her credit card was missing, so I bought dinner, and then she discovered that her credit card wallet was actually just on the floor of Farmsister’s car, so it was a quick resolution at least. But I got not one but Two wacky stories, so I’m gonna tell them one at a time. And here’s the first one: cut; discussions of small town life and a lady who got murdered We were driving through Johnsonville, which is part of the other town that makes up the school district where Mom taught and where we all went to school. (Two towns, and I grew up in the other one.) We passed a nondescript house off to the left, and Mom, who’d been pointing out local bits of interest and such, said, “Oh, and that’s the house where that lady got murdered, you know?” “What?” I had no idea what she was talking about, and Farmsister, weirdly, also didn’t seem to-- she’s much more tuned-in to local gossip. “You know,” Mom said, “that lady! Whose ex-husband poisoned her with rat poison?” It transpired that this “lady” was one year behind Farmsister in school, which was why FS had not recognized the anecdote from the description. That was the part she proceeded to object to. How could someone younger than her possibly be described as that lady . I had to break it to her that she’s pretty middle-aged now. (I have to do math. 36, girl, you’re 36. Strangers would definitely describe you as that lady .) I was objecting that there’d been a local murder I hadn’t heard about. Apparently it took a year for toxicology to figure out that it was strychnine and not a drug overdose. No one has been charged. She was living with an ex-husband, who has since been arrested for assaulting a different partner in the ensuing time. And she was dating an unsavory fellow, who’s also under suspicion. People have lawn signs, “Justice for [Firstname]”, and one of the local diners donates a portion of the proceeds from every one they sell of a particular entree toward the Justice For [Lady] Fund. One of Mom’s knitting bee buddies taught her daughter (of whom she did not have custody) at the time. It’s very sad, clearly her life was not going super well, and law enforcement doesn’t seem to think her life worth very much. But my sister is mostly just offended by being considered A Lady. OK that one wasn’t as funny in the retelling as it was at the time. Trust me, three of us in a car and my sister, driving, going slightly shrill as she repeats “That lady ?” and my mom continues to unperturbedly fill us in on everyone she knows who knows the participants, as we drive up to the bridge over the Hoosic River, was extremely my family dynamic, though usually it’s louder because usually there are more of us in any given place.

text: A Sad Story 6.2.2021 (Don't worry, no one died!) 15 years ago, when I was 27, I was talking to my mom. We were reminiscing about my grandfather who used to live in Washington PA. We were remembering when we all flew out to visit our grandparents one year (my sisters and I were still really little), and stayed for a few days. I didn't remember very much about the stay, but there was one thing I remember very clearly: getting to sit in grandpa's car. It wasn't just any car, though. It was a DeLorean. That's right, the Back To The Future car! We all got to take a turn playing with those gull wing doors and pretending to drive it. It was the best thing ever! My mom oh so casually says "Grandpa left you that car in his will." My twin and I were like "whut?" Mom replies "Grandpa put it in his will that when you turned 21, the car was yours," Then our mom dropped the The Bomb and said "Your cousin was supposed to maintain it for you until you turned 21, then have it brought down..." And this my friends, is where The Sad comes in. She says, "But he wrecked it and sold it for parts." It felt like my soul got slapped into the void. We joke about it now, but I'm not gonna lie, even though 15 years have passed, I'm still just a wee bit salty about it!

My dark humor at its finest: Before I start the story, I need to explain that everyone involved is ok and no relationship was ruined and no one got seriously hurt. Just a little hurt. And this is all true. So I have an older sister named Christine and she hates her name. Basically all her life when people found out her name, the would sing it like how it’s sung in the Phantom of the opera. You know, 🎶 Christine, Christine, Christine🎶. So she hates phantom of the opera, but I love it. Like obsessively. So her husband, my brother-in-law, worked at a pest control company and one day one of the chemical containers blew up in his hand and got all over him. And of course we’re all worried because he’s in the ER. Eventually I get tired of waiting, so I call up my sister Christine to check on how he’s doing. She says that he’s fine, he didn’t lose his eye sight or have any serious burns. As it turns out, the chemicals only got on HALF of his face and so it’s all red and swollen right now but only on HALF of his face. And I, having a sick sense of humor (and knowing he’ll be alright in a couple of days) say to her “I guess that makes you guys the Phantom and Christine.” To which she yells “HE’S NOT THAT DEFORMED! ITS JUST LIKE A BAD SUNBURN!” To which I replied “so you guys are Movie Phantom and Christine.” She then lets out an exasperated sigh and hangs up on me.

To be fair, the horse was smacking him with its head first (dad) · Being bitten by a horse then biting it back (uncle 1) · Falling due to ice and rupturing 4 vertebrae (dad) · Getting a small hole punched into your lip with a metal rod because sibling is a jerk (dad) · Breaking a tooth on pizza (dad) · Eye ulcer (me) · Getting tossed through a wall while practicing kung fu moves (uncle 2) · Getting tattooed in the face with a volleyball because uncle tried spiking it during a game and probably got a concussion (me) · Accidentally flipped a truck while trying to avoid an oncoming collision and landed upside down in a lake. This is my version of Mother's apple pie. She made deep dish apple pies and I never saw her use a recipe. It seemed like she just peeled and sliced and voila, there was our pie! Well, I peeled and sliced and here is my pie. Perhaps my dish was not deep enough? I had an aunt who hated making pies, but she married a man who loved eating pie. When she and my uncle first got married, she had reluctantly agreed that she would make him two pies per year. She was not, he told her, to try to slip pies from a bakery past him. Now my aunt could do many things perfectly. She was a fine artist and needlewoman, she headed committees both civic and charitable, but she never seemed to make any pies and was always several years in arrears and my uncle would tease her about it. When her son grew up and married, his wife enjoyed baking, so she and my aunt made a plan- My cousin's wife prepared several pies. Instead of baking them, however, she popped them into the freezer. Their plan was that my aunt would pull her daughter in law's frozen pies out of the freezer, put them into her own oven and serve them to my uncle. He would smell the baking pie, they thought, and he'd naturally assume that my aunt had made it herself and this way she would eventually get caught up. But my uncle was wilier than they had counted on. He discovered the stored pies in the freezer and after he enjoyed eating them, he informed them that simply baking a pie was not enough. The bargain, he told them, was that my aunt would make the pies herself from scratch. My aunt and uncle have been gone a long time, but wherever Uncle Bud is, he is still keeping count of the pie-less years and Aunt Lois is bemoaning the fact that she is falling farther and farther behind in her quota of pies.

When my twin and I were four, Mom was playing around with the old Polaroid, and we were taking pictures outside. We had several cats, and one of them photobombed our group picture with its butt. There we were, sitting on top of the picnic table, all smiles and grins, and right there next to our younger sister is the cat showing off its Eye of Sauron. My Mom cracked up, and from that day on, when ever we took any kind of photo, one of us would always shout "Say 'Cat Butt' !" 38 years later and we are still saying it for every photo! Where am I going with this? Well, Mom's birthday is coming up, and I just bought this off Wish. It should arrive just in time!
